info.title: Operator API
info.description: REST API for managing and running Operator server system.

get.summary: Get all active agents
get.description: Returns a list of all currently running agents with their status and details.

get.summary: Get details for a single agent by ID
get.description: Returns full details for a specific agent, including all tracked state.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: The agent ID to look up

post.description:
Clears the review state and signals the agent to continue.
The agent must be in `awaiting_input` status with a pending review.

post.summary: Focus the terminal session of a running agent in its session wrapper.
post.description:
The web UI's launch panel calls this for **cmux** launches: cmux exposes no
browser URL scheme, so the operator control plane (which runs inside cmux)
shells out to `cmux focus-workspace` for the agent's saved workspace ref to
bring its pane to the foreground. Other wrappers are unsupported here — VS
Code focuses through its extension's URI handler, and tmux/zellij are
display-only in the UI, so it never calls this for them.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: The agent ID whose session to focus

400.description: Wrapper unsupported, or no session refs to focus

post.description:
Signals the agent that the review was rejected with feedback.
The agent should re-do the work based on the rejection reason.

post.summary: Raise an external alert as an investigation (INV) ticket.
post.description:
Creates an investigation through the same embedded-template path as
[`create`], folding the alert's `source`/`severity` into the ticket values.
Powers the AGNT `operator-alert` node.

get.summary: List all collections
Get the currently active collection
Get a single collection by name
Get the current configuration
get.description:
Returns the full operator configuration as a JSON object. The body is left
opaque in the OpenAPI spec because the `Config` tree is large and no client
consumes its OpenAPI schema (the TS `Config` type is generated separately by
ts-rs).

200.description: Current configuration as a JSON object

put.summary: Update configuration and save to disk

200.description: Updated configuration as a JSON object

get.summary: List all configured delegators

post.summary: Create a delegator from a detected LLM tool
post.description: Pre-populates delegator fields from the detected tool, requiring minimal input.

post.summary: Import an `AgentProfile` as a new delegator.
post.description:
The shared-core fields Operator can't model and the opaque `x_agnt` bag are
preserved on the created delegator so a later export round-trips losslessly.

get.summary: Get a single delegator by name
Export a delegator as a portable `AgentProfile` (`agent-profile.json`).
get.description:
The shared core plus the Operator-namespaced `x_operator` bag; any opaque
AGNT (`x_agnt`) and shared-core carry the delegator holds are restored so the
profile is a lossless interchange artifact.

get.summary: Health check endpoint
GET `/api/v1/integrations`
get.description:
Returns the catalog of advertised integrations across every vertical, each
with its docs link and official support status (`proto` | `alpha` | `beta` |
`ga`).

200.description: Vertical integration catalog with support status

get.summary: List all issue types
Get a single issue type by key
List all steps for an issue type
Get a single step by name

404.description: Issue type or step not found
Issue type or step not found

get.summary: Preview the workflow for an issue type (no ticket required).
get.description:
Renders the issue type's step structure into a Claude dynamic workflow using
placeholder field values. Used by the UI to visualize an issue type's
workflow shape.

put.description:
Write or upsert a kanban provider+project section into `config.toml`.
Does NOT receive the actual secret — only the env var name (`api_key_env`).

post.description:
List available projects/teams for the given provider using ephemeral
credentials. No persistence side effects.

get.summary: GET /`api/v1/kanban/providers`
get.description:
Returns the catalog of supported kanban providers (Jira, Linear, GitHub),
each flagged with whether it is already configured. This is the shared
source of truth for the web `/#/kanban` list view and the VS Code
onboarding picker.

post.description:
Set kanban env vars on the server process for the current session so
subsequent `from_config()` calls find the API key. Returns a
`shell_export_block` with placeholder values for the client to display.

200.description: Session env vars set; returns a shell export block

post.description:
Validate credentials against the live provider API without persisting
anything. Auth failures return `valid: false` with an `error` string
rather than a 4xx/5xx status so clients can display errors inline.

get.summary: GET /`api/v1/kanban/:provider/:project_key/issuetypes`
get.description:
Returns kanban issue types from the persisted catalog for a given provider/project.
Falls back to fetching live from the provider if no catalog exists.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Kanban provider name (e.g. jira, linear, github)

500.description: Failed to read catalog or fetch from provider

post.description: Refreshes the local kanban issue type catalog from the provider.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Kanban provider name (e.g. jira, linear, github)

get.summary: List detected LLM tools with model aliases
Get the current default LLM tool and model

put.summary: Set the global default LLM tool and model

get.summary: MCP descriptor endpoint
get.description:
Returns metadata for registering operator with an MCP-capable client.
The transport URL is derived from the request Host header so it reflects
the actual running port; the stdio entrypoint reflects this binary's path.

get.summary: List all model servers (user-declared + implicit builtins)
The catalog of supported model-server kinds (single source of truth).
List the models a *provider kind* offers, via a live probe.
get.description:
Resolves to the declared instance of that kind (if the user has one) else a
transient instance built from the kind's probe defaults — so the Model
Providers catalog can show connection state + live models for every supported
provider without first declaring one. `reachable` doubles as "connected".

get.summary: Get a single model server by name

put.summary: Update an existing user-declared model server

delete.summary: Delete a user-declared model server by name
delete.description: Implicit builtin servers cannot be deleted.

get.summary: List the models a server offers, via a live probe of its inference endpoint.
get.description:
The probe doubles as a reachability check — `reachable: false` with an `error`
when the endpoint is unreachable or rejects the request.

get.summary: List all configured projects with analysis data

200.description: List of projects with analysis data

post.summary: Create an ASSESS ticket for a project

get.summary: Get kanban board data with tickets grouped by status column
get.description:
Returns tickets organized into four columns: queue, running, awaiting, done.
Tickets are sorted by priority within each column, then by timestamp (FIFO).

post.description: Sets the queue paused state to true, stopping automatic ticket launches.
Sets the queue paused state to false, resuming automatic ticket launches.

get.summary: Get queue status with ticket counts
get.description: Returns counts of tickets in each state plus breakdown by type.

post.description:
Fetches issues from configured external kanban providers (Jira, Linear, etc.)
and creates local tickets in the queue.

Fetches issues from a single provider/project combination and creates
local tickets in the queue.

get.summary: List the canonical status sections with health + child rows.
get.description:
Returns all sections (with a `met` flag) rather than hiding unmet ones, so
the web UI can render every section and style locked ones. The section logic
is injected by the binary; without a provider (lib-only/test) this is empty.

get.summary: List all discovered skills across LLM tools
Get service status with registry info

post.summary: Create a new ticket from a template and write it to the queue.
post.description:
Reuses the same [`TicketCreator`] the CLI (`operator create`) and MCP
(`operator_create_ticket`) use, so a ticket created over HTTP is identical to
one created on any other surface. Powers the AGNT `operator-create-ticket`
node.

get.summary: Get full ticket details by ID
get.description:
Returns complete ticket data including content, metadata, step history,
and session information. Searches queue, in-progress, and completed directories.

post.summary: Launch a ticket from the queue
post.description:
Claims the ticket, sets up worktree if needed, generates the LLM command,
and returns all details needed to execute in an external terminal (VS Code, etc.).

put.description:
Moves a ticket between queue directories based on the target status.
Valid transitions: queued, running, awaiting, done.

post.summary: Report step completion from opr8r wrapper
post.description:
Called by the opr8r wrapper when an LLM command completes.
Returns next step info and whether to auto-proceed.

post.summary: Export a ticket to a Claude dynamic workflow.
post.description:
Resolves the ticket (searching queue, in-progress, and completed), looks up
its issue type in the registry, and returns the rendered `.js` plus a
suggested filename.

404.description: Ticket or issue type not found

get.summary: List the workflow export formats operator can emit.
get.description:
Returns each [`WorkflowFormat`] with its label, file extension, support
status, and docs link — derived from `WorkflowFormat::ALL` joined to the
`Workflows` catalog vertical. Lets UIs render a format picker for the
`format` query param accepted by export/preview.

session_context_ref.description: Session context reference (e.g. cmux workspace, zellij session)

session_pane_ref.description: Session pane reference (e.g. cmux surface, zellij pane)

session_window_ref.description: Session window reference ID (e.g. cmux window, tmux session)

session_wrapper.description: Which session wrapper is in use: "tmux", "vscode", "cmux", or "zellij"

ticket_type.description: Ticket type: FEAT, FIX, INV, SPIKE

AgentDetailResponse.description: Full details for a single agent

paired.description: Whether this is a paired (interactive) agent

pr_url.description: PR URL if created during "pr" step

session_wrapper.description: Which session wrapper is in use: "tmux", "vscode", "cmux", or "zellij"

ticket_type.description: Ticket type: FEAT, FIX, INV, SPIKE

worktree_path.description: Path to the git worktree for this ticket

AgentProfile.description:
A canonical cross-tool agent definition (serialized as `agent-profile.json`).

Shared core (`name`/`provider`/`model`/`system_prompt`/`skills`/`mcp_servers`/
`tools`) plus namespaced extension bags. `x_operator` is typed (Operator owns
those fields); `x_agnt` and `x_openai` are opaque per-platform pass-throughs.

mcp_servers.description: MCP server names. Preserved opaquely across import.

oneOf.description:
Declarative reference to a remote, named agent (AGNT, `OpenAI`, ...).
`None` = a locally launchable agent, not bound to a remote platform.

skills.description: Named skills. Preserved opaquely across import.

system_prompt.description:
System prompt. Operator has no first-class system prompt, so this is
preserved opaquely across import (see [`Delegator::unmapped_core`]).

tools.description: Tool names. Preserved opaquely across import.

x_agnt.description:
AGNT-owned extension fields, opaque (`memory`, `assignedWorkflows`,
`creditLimit`, ...). Operator never interprets this — pure pass-through.

x_openai.description:
OpenAI-owned extension fields, opaque (`instructions`, `tools`,
`tool_resources`, `metadata`, thread refs, ...). Mirror of `x_agnt` for a
second platform — never interpreted. This field is the whole per-tool cost
of adding `OpenAI`: a passthrough bag, no mapping logic.

oneOf.description:
Operator-owned extension fields (typed). `None` when the agent carries no
Operator-specific configuration.

AssessTicketResponse.description: Response from creating an ASSESS ticket

ticket_path.description: Path to the created ticket file

oneOf.description: Descriptive workflow hints (present for hosted collections).

CreateAlertRequest.description: Request to raise an external alert as an investigation ticket.

filename.description: The investigation ticket filename written to the queue.

CreateDelegatorFromToolRequest.description:
Request to create a delegator from a detected LLM tool

Pre-populates delegator fields from the detected tool, requiring minimal input.
If `name` is omitted, auto-generates as `"{tool_name}-{model}"`.
If `model` is omitted, uses the tool's first model alias.

model.description: Model alias to use (e.g., "opus"). If omitted, uses the tool's first model alias.

model_server.description: Name of a declared `ModelServer`. `None` means use the `llm_tool`'s implicit vendor default.

name.description: Custom delegator name. If omitted, auto-generates as `"{tool_name}-{model}"`.

CreateDelegatorRequest.description: Request to create a new delegator

llm_tool.description: LLM tool name (must match a detected tool)

model_server.description: Name of a declared `ModelServer`. `None` means use the `llm_tool`'s implicit vendor default.

oneOf.description:
Declarative reference to a remote, named agent (AGNT, `OpenAI`, ...). When
set, the delegator is export-only and cannot be launched locally.

CreateIssueTypeRequest.description: Request to create a new issue type

CreateModelServerRequest.description: Request to create a new model server

api_key_env.description: Name of an env var providing the API key

base_url.description: Base URL of the inference endpoint

name.description: Unique name for this model server

CreateTicketRequest.description: Request to create a new ticket from a template.

project.description: Project the ticket targets (filled into the template's `project` value).

summary.description: One-line summary (filled into the template's `summary` value).

template.description: Template type key (feature, fix, spike, investigation, task).

values.description:
Additional Handlebars values for the template. Explicit `project`/
`summary` fields take precedence over the same keys here.

filename.description: The ticket filename written to the queue.

path.description: The absolute path the ticket was written to.

DefaultLlmResponse.description: Response with the current default LLM tool and model

model.description: Default model alias (empty string if not set)

tool.description: Default tool name (empty string if not set)

DelegatorLaunchConfig.description:
Launch configuration for a delegator

Controls how the delegator launches agents. Optional fields use tri-state
semantics: `None` = inherit from global config, `Some(true/false)` = override.

create_branch.description: Whether to create a git branch for the ticket (None = default behavior)

docker.description: Run in docker container (None = use global `launch.docker.enabled`)

operator_relay.description: Override global relay auto-inject MCP setting per-delegator (None = use global setting)

prompt_prefix.description: Prompt text to prepend before the generated step prompt

prompt_suffix.description: Prompt text to append after the generated step prompt

use_worktrees.description: Override global `git.use_worktrees` per-delegator (None = use global setting)

DelegatorLaunchConfigDto.description:
Launch configuration DTO for delegators

Optional fields use tri-state semantics: `None` = inherit global config,
`Some(true/false)` = explicit override per-delegator.

create_branch.description: Whether to create a git branch for the ticket (None = default behavior)

docker.description: Run in docker container (None = use global `launch.docker.enabled`)

operator_relay.description: Override global relay auto-inject MCP setting per-delegator (None = use global setting)

prompt_prefix.description: Prompt text to prepend before the generated step prompt

prompt_suffix.description: Prompt text to append after the generated step prompt

model_server.description: Name of a declared `ModelServer`. `None` means use the `llm_tool`'s implicit vendor default.

oneOf.description:
Declarative reference to a remote, named agent (AGNT, `OpenAI`, ...). When
set, the delegator is export-only and cannot be launched locally.

DetectedTool.description: A detected CLI tool (e.g., claude binary)

min_version.description: Minimum required version for Operator compatibility

version_ok.description: Whether the installed version meets the minimum requirement

ExternalIssueTypeSummary.description: Summary of an issue type from an external kanban provider (Jira, Linear)

GithubCredentials.description:
Ephemeral GitHub Projects credentials supplied by a client during onboarding.

The token must have `project` (or `read:project`) scope. A repo-only token
(the kind used for `GITHUB_TOKEN` and operator's git provider) will be
rejected at validation time with a friendly "lacks `project` scope" error.

token.description: GitHub PAT, fine-grained PAT, or app installation token

GithubProjectInfoDto.description: A GitHub Project v2 surfaced during onboarding for project picker UIs.

node_id.description: `GraphQL` node ID (e.g., `PVT_kwDOABcdefg`) — used as the project key

GithubSessionEnv.description: GitHub Projects session env body — includes the actual secret to set in env.

projects.description: All Projects v2 visible to the token (across viewer + organizations)

resolved_env_var.description:
The env var name the validated token came from. Used by clients to
display "Connected via `OPERATOR_GITHUB_TOKEN`" so users can rotate the
right token. See Token Disambiguation in the kanban github docs.

user_id.description: Authenticated user's numeric `databaseId` as a string (used as `sync_user_id`)

directory_id.description: Non-reversible fingerprint of the working root's canonical path.

directory_name.description: Top-level directory name of the operator working root (e.g. "acme").

IntegrationCatalogEntryDto.description:
One advertised integration: its vertical, identity, docs link, and support
status.

docs_url.description: Absolute docs URL, or `null` if undocumented.

readme_badge.description: Whether this entry carries a curated README badge.

slug.description: Stable entry slug within the vertical (e.g. "jira", "anthropic-api").

vertical_label.description: Human label for the vertical (e.g. "Kanban Provider").

IssueTypeResponse.description: Response for a single issue type

IssueTypeSummary.description: Summary response for listing issue types

JiraCredentials.description:
Ephemeral Jira credentials supplied by a client during onboarding.

These are never persisted to disk by the onboarding endpoints that take
this struct — the actual secret stays in the env var named in
`api_key_env` once set via `/api/v1/kanban/session-env`.

email.description: Atlassian account email for Basic Auth

JiraSessionEnv.description: Jira session env body — includes the actual secret to set in env.

KanbanBoardResponse.description: Kanban board response with tickets grouped by column

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last data refresh

total_count.description: Total ticket count across all columns

KanbanIssueTypeResponse.description: A synced kanban issue type from the persisted catalog.

id.description: Provider-specific ID (Jira type ID, Linear label ID)

source_kind.description: What this type represents in the provider ("issuetype" or "label")

KanbanProviderCatalogEntry.description:
One supported kanban provider, as advertised by
`GET /api/v1/kanban/providers`.

This is the single source of truth (derived from
`KanbanProviderType::ALL`) that the web `/#/kanban` list view and the VS
Code onboarding picker both render against, so the available options can't
drift between surfaces.

configured.description: Whether at least one instance of this provider is already configured.

description.description: One-line connect description shown next to the provider.

icon.description: VS Code codicon hint (rendered as `$(icon)` in the picker).

setup_url.description: Credential/token page opened when the user chooses to configure it.

KanbanProviderKind.description: Which kanban provider an onboarding request targets.

skipped.description: Ticket IDs that were skipped (already exist)

KanbanTicketCard.description: A ticket card for the kanban board

status.description: Current status: queued, running, awaiting, completed

ticket_type.description: Ticket type: FEAT, FIX, INV, SPIKE

delegator.description: Named delegator to use (takes precedence over provider/model)

model.description: Model to use (e.g., "sonnet", "opus") — legacy fallback when no delegator

model_server.description:
Ad-hoc model server to target (e.g. "ollama-local") — legacy fallback when
no delegator. Injects the server's base URL / API key env at spawn.

provider.description: LLM provider to use (e.g., "claude") — legacy fallback when no delegator

resume_session_id.description: Existing session ID to resume (for continuing from where it left off)

retry_reason.description: Feedback for relaunch (what went wrong on previous attempt)

yolo_mode.description: Run in YOLO mode (auto-accept all prompts)

agent_id.description: Agent ID assigned to this launch

session_context_ref.description: Session context reference (e.g. cmux workspace, zellij session)

session_id.description: Session UUID for the LLM tool

session_window_ref.description: Session window reference ID (e.g. cmux window, tmux session)

session_wrapper.description: Which session wrapper was used: "tmux", "vscode", or "cmux"

terminal_name.description: Terminal name to use (same value as `tmux_session_name`)

tmux_session_name.description: Tmux session name for attaching (same value as `terminal_name`, kept for backward compat)

working_directory.description: Working directory (worktree if created, else project path)

LinearCredentials.description: Ephemeral Linear credentials supplied by a client during onboarding.

LinearSessionEnv.description: Linear session env body — includes the actual secret to set in env.

LinearTeamInfoDto.description: A Linear team exposed to onboarding clients for project selection.

ListKanbanProjectsRequest.description: Request to list projects/teams from a provider using ephemeral creds.

ListKanbanProjectsResponse.description: Response wrapper for list-projects (wrapped for utoipa compatibility).

tools.description: Detected CLI tools with model aliases and capabilities

McpDescriptorResponse.description: MCP server descriptor for client discovery

openapi_url.description: URL of the OpenAPI spec for reference

server_name.description: Server name used in MCP registration (e.g. "operator")

oneOf.description:
Stdio transport entrypoint. Present when `[mcp].stdio_advertised = true`.
Clients may spawn this as a subprocess instead of using `transport_url`.

transport_url.description: Full URL of the MCP SSE transport endpoint

ModelEntry.description: A single model offered by a server (from a live probe).

display_name.description: Display name when the server provides one

ModelServerKindEntry.description: A model-server kind from the shared catalog (single source of truth).

brand_icon.description:
Brand-icon basename (e.g. "ollama") for surfaces that render vendor
logos, or `None` to fall back to the `icon` codicon.

category.description:
Provider-class slug grouping kinds within the Model Provider vertical
("first-party" or "gateway"). Distinct from `is_builtin`. (Field name
kept as `category` for wire/TS stability.)

connectable.description:
Whether operator can probe this provider from built-in defaults (it has a
`default_base_url`) without the user first declaring an instance.

default_api_key_env.description:
Default env var the probe reads to authenticate (e.g. `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`),
or `None` when no key is needed by default.

default_base_url.description:
Default inference base URL used to probe this provider's models when no
instance declares one, or `None` for bring-your-own-endpoint kinds.

is_builtin.description: Whether this is an implicit vendor builtin (always present, not deletable)

ModelServerModelsResponse.description: Response listing the models a server offers, plus reachability.

ModelServerResponse.description: Response for a single model server

api_key_env.description: Name of an env var providing the API key (e.g., `OLLAMA_API_KEY`)

base_url.description: Base URL of the inference endpoint (e.g., `http://localhost:11434`)

extra_env.description: Additional environment variables set when spawning agents that use this server

user_declared.description: Whether this is a user-declared server (true) or an implicit builtin (false)

ModelServersResponse.description: Response listing all model servers (declared + implicit builtins)

NextStepInfo.description: Information about the next step in the workflow

OperatorOutput.description:
Standardized agent output for progress tracking and step transitions.

Agents output a status block in their response which is parsed into this structure.
Used for progress tracking, loop detection, and intelligent step transitions.

exit_signal.description: Agent signals done with step (true) or more work remains (false)

files_modified.description: Number of files changed this iteration

status.description: Current work status: `in_progress`, complete, blocked, failed

summary.description: Brief description of work done (max 500 chars)

tasks_completed.description: Number of sub-tasks completed this iteration

tests_status.description: Test suite status: passing, failing, skipped, `not_run`

ProjectSummary.description: Summary of a project with analysis data

commands.description: Available command names (start, dev, test, etc.)

exists.description: Whether the project directory exists on disk

QueueByType.description: Ticket counts by type for queue status

paused.description: Whether the queue is currently paused

QueueStatusResponse.description: Queue status response with ticket counts

RejectReviewRequest.description: Request to reject an agent's review

reason.description: Reason for rejection (feedback for the agent)

RemoteAgentRef.description:
A declarative reference to a remote, named agent hosted by another platform.

`platform` is the hosting service (`"agnt"`, `"openai"`) — deliberately
distinct from the core `provider`/`llm_tool` (the model or coding CLI). These
agents are API/memory-native and live on the remote side; Operator has no
runtime client for them, so a delegator carrying one is **export-only** and
cannot be launched locally (see the guard in `delegator_resolution`).

id.description: Platform-native agent identifier (e.g. an AGNT agent UUID, an `OpenAI` `asst_…` id).

RowActionDto.description:
A browser-openable action on a status section row (e.g. "Open Web UI",
"Swagger"). Only URL-style actions surface to the web UI; TUI-only actions
(toggles, env edits) are omitted.

label.description: Display label for the action button/link.

SectionDto.description: A status section with its health and child rows.

met.description:
Whether all prerequisites are met. Sections are always returned (the web
UI styles unmet ones as locked) rather than hidden by progressive disclosure.

prerequisites.description: Section ids that must be Green before this section is usable.

SectionRowDto.description: A child row within a status section.

actions.description: Browser-openable actions for this row (links shown in the web UI).

brand_icon.description:
Optional vendor-brand basename (e.g. "ollama"). When set, the web UI
renders `/icons/{brand_icon}.svg` instead of the semantic `icon`.

depth.description:
Nesting depth within the section (1 = direct child, 2 = grandchild).
Lets clients rebuild the tree (e.g. LLM tools → model aliases).

id.description:
Stable, section-scoped row id. Clients use it as a tree key and to route
row-specific commands without matching on the (mutable) display label.
Dynamic rows carry their entity key (issue-type key, project name);
static rows carry a fixed slug (e.g. "git-token").

SetDefaultLlmRequest.description: Request to set the global default LLM tool and model

tool.description: Tool name (must match a detected tool, e.g., "claude")

SetKanbanSessionEnvRequest.description:
Request to set kanban-related env vars on the server for the current
session so subsequent `from_config` calls find the API key.

SetKanbanSessionEnvResponse.description:
Response from setting session env vars.

`shell_export_block` uses `<your-token>` placeholders, NOT the actual
secret — it is meant for the user to copy into their shell profile.

env_vars_set.description: Names (not values) of env vars that were set in the server process.

shell_export_block.description:
Multi-line `export FOO="<your-token>"` block for the user to copy
into `~/.zshrc` / `~/.bashrc`.

file_path.description: Full path to the skill file

directory_id.description: Non-reversible fingerprint of the working root's canonical path.

directory_name.description: Top-level directory name of the operator working root (e.g. "acme").

StdioCommand.description:
Stdio entrypoint advertised in the descriptor when
`[mcp].stdio_advertised = true`. Clients use this to spawn operator
as an MCP subprocess instead of (or alongside) the SSE transport.

command.description: Absolute path to the operator binary (the same binary serving this descriptor)

cwd.description:
Working directory the client should set when spawning. Defaults to the
operator process's current working directory.

StepCompleteRequest.description: Request to report step completion (from opr8r wrapper)

duration_secs.description: Duration of the step in seconds

exit_code.description: Exit code from the LLM command

output_sample.description: Sample of the output (first N chars for debugging)

output_schema_errors.description: List of validation errors (if `output_valid` is false)

output_valid.description: Whether output validation passed (if schema was specified)

session_id.description: Session ID from the LLM session

auto_proceed.description: Whether to automatically proceed to the next step

iteration_count.description: How many times this step has run (for circuit breaker)

next_command.description: Command to execute for the next step (opr8r wrapped)

oneOf.description: Information about the next step (if any)

output_valid.description: Whether `OperatorOutput` was successfully parsed from agent output

should_iterate.description: Agent has more work (`exit_signal=false`) - indicates iteration needed

SupportStatus.description: Official support / maturity level of an advertised integration.

SyncKanbanIssueTypesResponse.description: Response from syncing kanban issue types from a provider.

TicketDetailResponse.description: Full ticket details including content and metadata

content.description: Full markdown content of the ticket

external_id.description: External issue ID from kanban provider

external_url.description: URL to the issue in the external provider

status.description: Current status: queued, running, awaiting, completed

ticket_type.description: Ticket type: FEAT, FIX, INV, SPIKE

supports_headless.description: Whether the tool can run in headless/non-interactive mode

supports_sessions.description: Whether the tool supports session continuity via UUID

UpdateIssueTypeRequest.description: Request to update an issue type

UpdateModelServerRequest.description:
Request to update an existing user-declared model server.

All fields except `kind` are replaced; `name` is taken from the path.

api_key_env.description: Name of an env var providing the API key

base_url.description: Base URL of the inference endpoint

UpdateTicketStatusRequest.description: Request to update a ticket's status

status.description: Target status: queued, running, awaiting, done

UpdateTicketStatusResponse.description: Response from updating a ticket's status

ValidateKanbanCredentialsRequest.description: Request to validate kanban credentials without persisting them.

ValidateKanbanCredentialsResponse.description:
Response from validating kanban credentials.

`valid: false` is returned for auth failures — never a 4xx/5xx HTTP
status — so clients can display `error` inline without exception handling.

WorkflowExportResponse.description: Response for exporting a ticket to a Claude dynamic workflow (`.js`).

issuetype_key.description: The issue type key that supplied the step structure.

suggested_filename.description: Suggested filename for saving the workflow (`<ticket-id>.workflow.js`).

ticket_id.description: The ticket the workflow was generated from.

WorkflowFormat.description: Which orchestration format `workflow export` should emit.

WorkflowFormatDto.description:
One workflow export format operator can emit, for `GET /api/v1/workflow-formats`.

A projection of [`WorkflowFormat`] joined to its `Workflows` catalog entry —
the single source of truth for the format's [`SupportStatus`] and docs. Lets
the UIs render a format picker without hardcoding the list.

docs_url.description: Absolute docs URL, or `null` if undocumented.

extension.description: File extension of the emitted artifact, no leading dot (e.g. "js", "json").

slug.description: Stable slug (e.g. "claude", "agnt") — the value the `format` query param takes.

status.description: Official support / maturity status (from the catalog).

WorkflowHintsDto.description: Descriptive workflow hints for a collection (v1: metadata only).

WorkflowPreviewResponse.description:
Response for a *preview* workflow generated from an issue type alone (no
concrete ticket). Used by the UI to visualize an issue type's workflow shape.

contents.description: The generated `.js` workflow source (placeholder ticket values).

issuetype_key.description: The issue type key the preview was generated from.

suggested_filename.description: Suggested filename for saving the preview (`<KEY>.preview.workflow.js`).

WriteGithubConfigBody.description: Body for writing a GitHub Projects v2 config section.

api_key_env.description:
Env var name where the project-scoped token is set
(default: `OPERATOR_GITHUB_TOKEN`). MUST be distinct from `GITHUB_TOKEN`
— see Token Disambiguation in the kanban github docs.

owner.description: GitHub owner login (user or org), used as the workspace key

sync_user_id.description: Numeric GitHub `databaseId` of the user whose items to sync

WriteJiraConfigBody.description: Body for writing a Jira project config section.

WriteKanbanConfigRequest.description:
Request to write or upsert a kanban config section.

This endpoint does NOT take the secret — only the env var NAME
(`api_key_env`). The secret is set via `/api/v1/kanban/session-env`.

WriteKanbanConfigResponse.description: Response after writing a kanban config section.

section_header.description:
Header of the top-level section that was upserted
(e.g., `[kanban.jira."acme.atlassian.net"]`)

WriteLinearConfigBody.description: Body for writing a Linear project/team config section.

XOperator.description:
The Operator-namespaced half of an [`AgentProfile`] — the fields a Delegator
carries that have no shared-core equivalent. AGNT ignores this bag; Operator
round-trips it losslessly.

oneOf.description:
Launch configuration (permission mode, flags, worktree/docker, prompt
wrapping, ...).

model_server.description: Name of a declared `ModelServer` (`None` = implicit vendor default).

tags.name: Status
tags.description: Canonical status sections (TUI / VS Code parity)

tags.name: Workflow
tags.description: Export tickets to Claude dynamic workflows

tags.name: ModelServers
tags.description: Model server (ollama, openai-compat, etc.) CRUD operations

tags.name: Queue
tags.description: Ticket queue board, status, and control

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Active agent tracking and review actions

tags.name: Kanban
tags.description: Kanban provider issue types and onboarding
